question: Create a `Logger` class. Its constructor should open a specified log file, and its destructor should close the file, ensuring proper resource management. Add a `logMessage(const std::string& message)` method.

test_cases.expected:
System initialized.
Processing batch job.
Batch job completed.

Data item 1 processed.
Data item 2 processed.
Data item 3 processed.

errors: The `logMessage` method takes `std::string message` by value. This creates an unnecessary copy of the message string for every log operation, which can lead to performance overhead, especially for large or frequent messages. Passing `const std::string&` would be more efficient.

The `logMessage` method uses `std::endl` to append a newline and flush the output buffer. While correct, flushing the buffer on every single log entry can be inefficient for high-volume logging scenarios. Using `'
'` instead and allowing the buffer to flush automatically (or explicitly flushing when truly necessary) would generally offer better performance.
